name: lfss
documentation:
# Lightweight File Storage Service (LFSS)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/lfss)](https://pypi.org/project/lfss/)

My experiment on a lightweight and high-performance file/object storage service.  
It stores small files and metadata in sqlite, large files in the filesystem.  
Tested on 2 million files, and it works fine... 

Usage: 
```sh
pip install lfss
lfss-user add <username> <password>
lfss-serve
```

By default, the data will be stored in `.storage_data`. 
You can change storage directory using the `LFSS_DATA` environment variable.

I provide a simple client to interact with the service: 
```sh
lfss-panel --open
```
Or, you can start a web server at `/frontend` and open `index.html` in your browser. 

The API usage is simple, just `GET`, `PUT`, `DELETE` to the `/<username>/file/url` path.  
Authentication is done via `Authorization` header with the value `Bearer <token>`, or through the `token` query parameter.  
You can refer to `frontend` as an application example, and `frontend/api.js` or `lfss/api/connector.py` for the API usage.

By default, the service exposes all files to the public for `GET` requests, 
but file-listing is restricted to the user's own files.  
Please refer to [docs/Permission.md](./docs/Permission.md) for more details on the permission system.

documentation: todo: add exclusive transaction option

functions.name: lfss.src.utils.ensure_uri_compnents
documentation: Ensure the path components are safe to use

functions.name: lfss.src.utils.debounce_async
documentation:
Debounce the async procedure, 
ensuring execution at least once every `max_wait` seconds.

functions.name: lfss.src.utils.format_last_modified
documentation:
Format the last modified time to the [HTTP standard format](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Last-Modified)
- last_modified_gmt: The last modified time in SQLite ISO 8601 GMT format: e.g. '2021-09-01 12:00:00'

functions.name: lfss.src.utils.now_stamp
documentation: Get the current timestamp, in seconds

functions.name: lfss.src.utils.parse_storage_size
documentation: Parse the file size string to bytes

functions.name: lfss.src.thumb.get_thumb
documentation:
returns [image bytes of thumbnail, mime type] if supported, 
or None if not supported. 
Raises FileNotFoundError if file does not exist

functions.name: lfss.src.server.get_current_user
documentation:
First try to get the user from the bearer token, 
if not found, try to get the user from the query parameter

functions.name: lfss.src.server.post_file
documentation: using form-data instead of raw body

modules.name: lfss.src.bounded_pool
documentation: Code modified from: https://github.com/mowshon/bounded_pool_executor

class_variables.name: lfss.src.datatype.FileReadPermission.UNSET
documentation: not set

class_variables.name: lfss.src.datatype.FileReadPermission.PUBLIC
documentation: accessible by anyone

class_variables.name: lfss.src.datatype.FileReadPermission.PROTECTED
documentation: accessible by any user

class_variables.name: lfss.src.datatype.FileReadPermission.PRIVATE
documentation: accessible by owner only (including admin)

class_variables.name: lfss.src.datatype.FileRecord.file_id
documentation: defines mapping from fmata to blobs.fdata

variables.name: lfss.src.config.LARGE_FILE_BYTES
documentation: 8MB

variables.name: lfss.src.config.MAX_MEM_FILE_BYTES
documentation: 128MB

variables.name: lfss.src.config.MAX_BUNDLE_BYTES
documentation: 512MB

variables.name: lfss.src.config.CHUNK_SIZE
documentation: 1MB chunks for streaming (on large files)

methods.name: lfss.src.database.FileConn.list_root_dirs
documentation: Efficiently list users' directories, if usernames is empty, list all users' directories.

methods.name: lfss.src.database.FileConn.list_path
documentation:
List all files and directories under the given path.  
This method is a handy way file browsing, but has limitaions:
- It does not support pagination
- It does not support sorting
- It cannot flatten directories
- It cannot list directories with details

methods.name: lfss.src.database.FileConn.get_path_record
documentation: Get the full record of a directory, including size, create_time, update_time, access_time etc.

methods.name: lfss.src.database.FileConn.delete_path_records
documentation: Delete all records with url starting with path

classes.name: lfss.src.database.Database

methods.name: lfss.src.database.Database.save_file
documentation:
Save a file to the database. 
Will check file size and user storage limit, 
should check permission before calling this method.
higher level database operations, mostly transactional

modules.name: lfss.cli.panel
documentation: A static file server to serve frontend panel

modules.name: lfss.cli.balance
documentation: Balance the storage by ensuring that large file thresholds are met.

modules.name: lfss.cli.vacuum
documentation: Vacuum the database and external storage to ensure that the storage is consistent and minimal.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.session
documentation: avoid creating a new session for each request.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.put
documentation: Uploads a file to the specified path.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.post
documentation:
Uploads a file to the specified path, 
using the POST method, with form-data/multipart.
file can be a path to a file on disk, or bytes.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.put_json
documentation: Uploads a JSON file to the specified path.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.get
documentation: Downloads a file from the specified path.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.get_stream
documentation: Downloads a file from the specified path, will raise PathNotFoundError if path not found.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.delete
documentation: Deletes the file at the specified path.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.get_metadata
documentation: Gets the metadata for the file at the specified path.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.list_path
documentation:
shorthand list with limited options, 
for large directories / more options, use list_files and list_dirs instead.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.set_file_permission
documentation: Sets the file permission for the specified path.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.move
documentation: Move file or directory to a new path.

methods.name: lfss.api.connector.Connector.whoami
documentation: Gets information about the current user.
